relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The letter’s theology becomes practical care for the holy ones.
Regular setting aside makes generosity ready and peaceful.
Approved carriers will bring the grace-gift to Jerusalem.
Paul may accompany the gift if fitting.
Travel plans move through Macedonia.
He hopes for more than a passing visit.
Time together depends on the Lord’s permission.
Ephesus remains his post until Pentecost.
A great effective door opens amid opposition.
Timothy should be received without fear.
Do not despise the young worker; send him in peace.
Apollos’ timing is honored rather than forced.
Wakefulness, firmness, courage, and strength are needed.
Everything must happen in love.
Stephanas’ household shows firstfruit service.
The community is asked to honor those who labor in service.
Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus fill the gap of presence.
They refresh spirits and deserve recognition.
Asia’s assemblies, Aquila, Prisca, and their house-assembly greet Corinth.
The siblings greet one another in holy affection.
Paul marks the greeting by his own hand.
Love for the Lord and the Marana tha cry close the warning.
Grace of Lord Jesus remains with them.
Paul ends with love for all in Messiah.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 16 brings the whole letter into practical love: shared money, travel, workers, courage, house-assemblies, grace, and love with all.
